tasks.prompt:
Check for new Bokio emails and auto-respond if substantive.

STEP 1 - Check inbox:
Run: onemessage inbox email --all --from "bokio" --fresh
Parse the output to find message IDs (leftmost number column). Read ~/.config/onemessage/bokio-last-seen.txt to get the last processed ID.

STEP 2 - Identify new messages:
Any message ID numerically HIGHER than last-seen is new. If none, stop silently.

STEP 3 - For each new message, read and classify:
Run: onemessage read email <id>
Strip HTML when reading. Classify:
- AUTO: subject contains "Tack för att du kontaktat oss" or "Ticket created" or "värderar din åsikt" or is a satisfaction survey
- SUBSTANTIVE: anything else from Bokio support that is a direct reply to the dispute

STEP 4a - If SUBSTANTIVE reply found:
Context: A settlement was reached on April 16 2026. Bokio agreed to waive the 2024 debt and restore account access for April 3 – July 2 2026. We are awaiting written confirmation. If Bokio sends a confirmation, acknowledge it warmly and note the dispute is closed. If Bokio sends anything that retracts the settlement or introduces new issues, respond firmly citing the settlement agreement (their email of April 16 2026) and demand they honour it.

Draft reply to /tmp/bokio_reply.txt, then:
onemessage reply email <id> -f /tmp/bokio_reply.txt

Create marker file:
echo "$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ) message_id=$id" > ~/.config/onemessage/bokio-substantive-received.txt

Ping Dennis:
onemessage me "Bokio: nytt substantiellt meddelande (ID [id]). Ämne: [subject]. Sammanfattning: [2 sentences]. Åtgärd vidtagen: [sent/acknowledged]."

STEP 4b - If AUTO-REPLY or survey only:
Update state only. No ping needed.

STEP 5 - Update state:
Write the highest new message ID to ~/.config/onemessage/bokio-last-seen.txt
